would say landing pages and like newsletters, like building newsletters for like...
They're buying like a functional output from you, which are landing pages and things like that.
And so essentially they come to you, they have a project, they're like launching something or something.
And then like you then execute or you do like a microsite like this, like MCP thing.
Yeah.
And...
It's a marketing effort.
Yeah.
It's more like, let's accelerate what we're doing.
And so we know that our content works.
We know our brand.
We know our target audience.
Like, we know that.
But now there's all this AI noise.
And now we suddenly just don't know what to do.
And so there's people that are just like, hey, like, I've been doing this for 20 years.
I'm the best SEO writer.
I'm the best life cycle marketer.
But it's not working anymore.
So, yeah, exactly.
So now we're just seeing those people that are extremely scared and just putting them into this bucket and then saying, hey, we're going to learn together.
We're going to take you with that.
We don't know this either.
But we're going to be committed to figuring it out.
And we will learn with you.
So that's how it's set up for now.
And what is the – go ahead.
I was going to say it's also like I would say more so than like do a workshop or a newsletter one off.
It's more so like give all of your content operations directly to us.
So every part of it from like programmatic landing pages, you know, social media, you know, ghost writing, things like that.
And then you can also use our platform to monitor all this stuff as well.
So that's kind of how – But that's like outsourcing.
Like to you, like outsourcing.
Function rather than building that capability in-house.
So is the idea that like if you've got that marketer of 20 years who has like, you know, a staff, they get rid of the staff and then they just like replace it with you guys?
Yeah, yeah, actually not.
We don't want to replace anyone.
So we definitely want to just be a learning resource.

And so like we've got a team of editors and writers that can help with copy, but then actually training the people and how they can do it themselves and like how they can build these workflows and like why they should use AI.
Like that's the biggest part of just like less so about, hey, let's just go make a thousand landing pages.
It's more so like ramp saying, hey, we're going to spend $40,000 a month.
We want you to manage every single part of content.
And so then we have to come and bring in each pod has like a director and then there's editors and then there's like managing editors and things like that.
So we're really set up like a publication and also an agency.
So like said, tons of reporters.
A lot of the people that are speaking with these people are former reporters.
That's an offshore more so just like, hey, like help us.

I guess what I'm trying to ask, though, is whether or not at some point Ramp pulls back and is like, cool, we got the lessons, we understand it now, we're going to do it ourselves, or if there's a constant, like you act as a kind of, you know, AI era PR agency that, again, serves that function and that Ramp would never build that capability in-house.
Yeah, I would say it's probably the second or the latter.
So I would say probably the latter effect.
That's how they usually approach us.
And we're still so new of like, we don't really know what will happen.
And like the last September was a six people company.
Now it's 80.
And so everyone's just kind of like figuring out what this is going to be.
But, you know, we're asking the right questions.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
Um, okay.
I think I get it.
Uh, is there anything else that I need to know or in terms of our deck and everything?
If you can see the deck, that would be very helpful.
Um, even if it's, you know, out of date, it just like provides some color and some background and
I tend to work better when I can synthesize a lot of information and like a lot of different perspectives.
So that's, that's useful.
And then, so in terms of like next steps, I'm happy to put together, you know, kind of like a high level quasi like proposal, just sort like a statement of like things, like things that could happen.
And then we can, you know, I can go back and forth a little bit more.
And then, and then, so, so let's, again, let's talk about this timeline, right?
So the one short term, we can do some posts on social, pretty straightforward, coin operated more or less.
But then there's the bigger question of like, what is that content like?
And is there sort of a longer term content, like arrangement partnership type of thing?
And then that would require a little bit more intentionality in terms of shaping that.
Are there any other work streams that you're thinking of or that I missed?
Yeah, no, I would say like the events, definitely doing like speaker series, doing these things.
know.
Thank
Outside of that newsletter, I lost the first newsletter yesterday, so it's the first one we've done.
I'll share with you the copy.
I'll share with you the deck.
I just want to send you a ton of information.
Yeah, please.
It's going to make a lot more sense.
Okay.
And it all kind of comes together.
So I'll send you a ton of stuff.

I'm very flexible, acing communication.
Whatever works for you, works for us.
And yeah, I just want to kind of make the picture a little bit better.
Do you guys use Slack?
Yes, we do.
Do you want to do like a shared channel?
Yeah, let's do that.
Let's get you on there.
Okay.
Can you just invite my email?
I'll send you my email.
I think you have my email already, but you want to invite my email.
That tends to be pretty productive for me.
And then, yeah, we can go back and forth on that.
Okay.
Yeah, let's start with that.
Let me send you some stuff.
I'll get you on Slack.
And we'll go back and forth.

But I think that'll be, it would be helpful for us both.
And what is the cadence of the events that you're planning to do?
Is that like monthly or bi-weekly or what do you think?
Yeah.
We do AI executive dinners like once a month-ish.

Like when HubSpot and Bound are these giant trade shows, we'll make them around for their like five.
Oh, yeah.
That will change.

So it's mostly at the growthx office.
We invite like maybe 20, 35, you know, CMOs and we do that.
And then last is like these, you know, other new thing that we're spending with these workshops.
So they'll be in person, they'll be virtual.

And then the goal is to have like a big user conference, I would say maybe October later this year, and bring it together in person, like 150 go-to-bucket leaders, do a small bootcamp, like get them in there to see to do these things.
So yeah, it's a little bit long, long term, but for now we're kind of building it and ideating what that's going to look like.
That's great.
Okay, cool.
Let's start there.

Sounds really cool.
Let's do it.
I'll get you on Slack.
This is awesome.

Yeah, thanks.
Appreciate it.
All right.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential collaboration between Chris Messina and GrowthX for upcoming AI workshop and broader content/influencer marketing initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX rapidly grew from 0 to $7M ARR in 7 months, bootstrapped to Series A ($12M from Madrona)](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [Seeking influencer support for Aug 1st AI workshop (300+ RSVPs already) and longer-term content collaboration](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
  - [Target audience: CMOs and senior executives in B2B/B2C companies looking to integrate AI into content operations](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1613.0)
  - [Flexible budget; open to various collaboration models (social posts, content creation, speaker series, etc.)](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=849.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX Company Overview

  - [Started as an agency in Sept 2022, now 80 employees across 22 countries](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [Offers AI-powered content operations services and education to enterprises](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1598.0)
  - [Minimum contract: $12K/month for comprehensive content strategy and execution](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1548.0)
  - [Sub-brands: The Output (publication), AI-led Growth (education platform)](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1598.0)

### Upcoming AI Workshop (Aug 1st)

  - [Focus on GEO (Google-Engineered Optimization) and AI SEO strategies](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
  - [300+ RSVPs already; aiming for broader reach through influencer marketing](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=897.0)
  - [Seeking thought leaders to amplify message and add credibility](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)

### Potential Collaboration Areas

  - [Short-term: Social media posts to promote the workshop](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0)
  - [Long-term: Co-created content series, possibly featuring case studies on innovative AI-driven companies](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [Speaker series or fireside chats at GrowthX's San Francisco office](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1475.0)
  - [Involvement in shaping The Output publication's content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1421.0)

### GrowthX's Target Audience

  - [Chief Marketing Officers and senior executives in B2B and B2C companies](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1613.0)
  - [Decision-makers capable of implementing org-wide AI adoption in content operations](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1613.0)
  - [Mix of AI-savvy leaders and those seeking guidance on getting started with AI](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1662.0)

### GrowthX's Service Model

  - [Acts as an external AI-era content agency, not replacing internal teams](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1812.0)
  - [Provides both execution (landing pages, newsletters) and education on AI workflows](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1715.0)
  - [Aims to be a learning partner, helping clients adapt to AI-driven content strategies](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1662.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Chris to draft a high-level proposal for potential collaboration areas](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1460.0)
  - [Bianca to share additional materials (pitch deck, newsletter copy) for context](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1984.0)
  - [Set up shared Slack channel for ongoing communication](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=2022.0)
  - [Explore possibilities for both short-term workshop promotion and longer-term content partnership](https://fathom.video/share/zxM8QyJc9feCxcGxJkL5quGT-L2yKqng?tab=summary&timestamp=1946.0)

Send Chris deck, newsletter copy, and additional background info on GrowthX
Invite Chris to GrowthX Slack, set up shared channel
